name: Product Frontend Backlog Next Selection Gate

plain_language_summary.title: > **白話說明**：
plain_language_summary.what_changes: This step only decides the next small frontend task. It does not implement UI changes.
plain_language_summary.why: After the command palette entrypoint fix, the backlog needs a fresh smallest-next-item decision instead of reopening a broad redesign.
plain_language_summary.user_visible_difference: Users will not see a product difference from 22.2 itself because this is a planning and selection gate.
plain_language_summary.does_not_change: This step does not change UI code, backend APIs, database schema, routes, note data, frontend default API target, Pi/Caddy/service runtime, Go runtime ownership, public exposure, AI/ML features, collections, or server-side UI preference persistence.

browser_evidence.browser_plugin: iab unavailable; used local Chrome plus Playwright fallback for read-only observation

settings_tab_deep_linking.finding: Settings tabs are local-only state; the URL does not preserve the selected tab and reload returns to Appearance.

prompt_builder_mobile_action_bar.finding: The action bar exists and is usable after scrolling, but mobile layout polish would require visual iteration beyond the smallest next item.

home_search_empty_state.finding: A no-result search is titled as search results but still says there are no notes, which reads like an empty library rather than an empty search result.

candidate_matrix.finding: When a search has zero matches, Home shows the generic no-notes message instead of a search-specific no-results message.
candidate_matrix.why_small: A narrow frontend-only copy/state branch can make the empty state match search/filter/archive context without new APIs, storage, schema, or routing.

candidate_matrix.finding: Settings tabs do not update the URL and reload returns to Appearance.
candidate_matrix.why_not_selected: Useful, but it introduces URL-state parsing and navigation behavior, which is broader than a copy-only Home empty state fix.

candidate_matrix.finding: At a 390x844 viewport, the action bar is below the first viewport and needs scrolling.
candidate_matrix.why_not_selected: This is visual/layout work that should use a dedicated visual iteration loop; it is broader than the next smallest copy/context fix.

selected_candidate.title: Home Search Empty State Context Copy
selected_candidate.scope: Frontend-only implementation to make Home empty-state text match current search/filter/archive context, starting with search no-results copy.
success_criteria: Searching for a term with zero matches no longer shows the generic 'no notes yet' message.
Default empty library state still says the library has no notes.
Filter/archive empty states can keep existing behavior unless the implementation can clarify them without broader workflow changes.
No backend API, DB schema, storage, routing, frontend default API target, Pi/Caddy/service, Go runtime, public exposure, AI/ML, collections, or server-side preference persistence changes.
verification_plan: Browser flow: search for a guaranteed no-match term and verify the empty state explains no search matches instead of saying the library has no notes.
selected_candidate.rollback_plan: Revert only HomePage empty-state copy/context changes if typecheck, build, tests, or browser flow fail.
stop_conditions: The fix requires a new backend endpoint, schema change, storage, route change, or data migration.
The fix turns into Settings, Prompt Builder, redesign, or mobile layout work.
The fix adds new actions that mutate data or change note/filter state beyond clearing existing UI context.
The empty library state becomes less clear.

not_authorized_by_22_2: New backend API or DB schema
Pi deploy or live service reload
AI/ML, collaboration, realtime, plugin platform, collections schema, or server-side UI preference persistence

allowed_next_step.title: Home Search Empty State Context Copy
allowed_next_step.scope: Implement only the selected frontend-only Home empty-state copy/context fix, then verify with typecheck, build, pytest, and browser flow.
